data.title: Investors move capital towards tokenized assets as DeFi sees a decline. Experts view the trend as market maturation, not panic.
data.description: Investors are moving money out of decentralized finance (DeFi) and into tokenized assets amid a market shift. This rotation reflects a growing maturity in the markets rather than a complete abandonment. The movement of capital highlights the transition occurring, with significant amounts of funds being reallocated. This change in strategy is seen by experts as a natural evolution in the market, signaling a shift in investment preferences.

data.title: Fed's Kashkari criticizes crypto as "utterly useless" vs AI, dismisses pro-stablecoin points as "buzzword salad."
data.description: Neel Kashkari, President of the Minneapolis Federal Reserve, recently stated that he believes cryptocurrency pales in comparison to AI, deeming it as "utterly useless." He also expressed skepticism towards arguments supporting stablecoins, referring to them as "a buzzword salad." Kashkari's remarks underscore his lack of enthusiasm for crypto technologies, emphasizing his preference for AI advancements.

data.title: Anthony Scaramucci questions if Bitcoin price accounts for Clarity Act passage. Views are divided. Some suggest it could be a 'buy the news' event post potential bill approval.
data.description: SkyBridge Capital's CEO, Anthony Scaramucci, questioned whether Bitcoin's price had already factored in the potential passage of the cryptocurrency market structure bill, known as the CLARITY Act. Scaramucci took to social media, asking if the legislation had been "baked into" Bitcoin's price. Various opinions surfaced in the replies, with one user referencing Coinbase's withdrawal leading to premature selling of BTC. However, there was speculation that if the bill passes, it could result in a "buy the news" event. The user suggested that due to a pessimistic setup, the Clarity Act was initially seen as a "sell the news" event, but premature selling caused by Coinbase's actions may have altered this perception. For more details, visit a financial news website.

data.title: QuickBooks named best payroll solution for UK small businesses by Better Business Advice, highlighting integration & compliance.
data.description: In a recent announcement on February 19, 2026, Better Business Advice has identified Intuit's QuickBooks Payroll as a superior payroll software choice for UK companies. This recognition underscores the trend of businesses valuing software solutions that streamline compliance, automate tasks, and enhance efficiency. It reflects the growing importance of adopting innovative technology in the modern business landscape. The acknowledgment highlights QuickBooks Payroll's exceptional performance in meeting the payroll needs of businesses in the UK, showcasing its effectiveness and reliability in this sector. The recognition from Better Business Advice serves as a testament to QuickBooks Payroll's position as a leading solution in the industry, offering UK businesses a robust platform for managing payroll with convenience and accuracy.
